zone_summary: GetDAGExecStatus now routes token-cache handling through a feature-flagged helper: delete cached tokens for terminal DAG states when the kill switch is off, keep updating tokens for non-terminal states or when the kill switch is on, and assert null SchedulerRunStatus input with a different exception type.

behavioral_changes.summary: When FLTTokenManagerSkipClearOnDagCompletion is disabled, scheduler-status reads now evict cached tokens for terminal DAG executions instead of refreshing them.
When the kill switch is disabled and the DAG is still non-terminal, scheduler-status reads continue refreshing the cached token instead of deleting it.
When FLTTokenManagerSkipClearOnDagCompletion is enabled, scheduler-status reads preserve the legacy behavior and update the cached token even for terminal DAG executions.
On the kill-switch-enabled path, UpdateCachedToken failures are caught and logged locally so GetDAGExecStatus can still return success.
SchedulerRunStatus.GetSchedulerRunStatus now enforces non-null input via Check.AssertArgumentNotNull, changing the null-input exception contract to RuntimeAssertException.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
Tracer.LogSanitizedMessage($"DAG Status for Iteration {iterationId} found to be {schedulerRunStatus.Status}");
UpdateOrDeleteTokenInTokenManager(tenantId, artifactId, iterationId, mwcToken, schedulerRunStatus.Status);
grounding_evidence.reason: The scheduler-status endpoint now delegates token handling to a status-aware helper after computing the DAG status.
The kill-switch branch explicitly preserves update behavior and wraps token updates in a local try/catch.
With the kill switch off, terminal DAG statuses now trigger token eviction.
With the kill switch off, non-terminal DAG statuses still refresh the cached token.
This helper defines the terminal-status partition used by the new delete-vs-update branch.
Contract-bearing comment documents the feature flag semantics and the legacy behavior it restores.
Null input validation now uses the platform assert helper instead of throwing ArgumentNullException directly.
Added unit test locks in delete-on-terminal behavior when the kill switch is disabled.
Added unit test locks in update-on-nonterminal behavior when the kill switch is disabled.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
mockFeatureFlighter
    .Setup(f => f.IsEnabled(
        FeatureNames.FLTTokenManagerSkipClearOnDagCompletion,
        It.IsAny<Guid?>(),
        It.IsAny<Guid?>(),
        It.IsAny<Guid?>()))
    .Returns(true);
...
mockITokenManager.Verify(
    x => x.UpdateCachedToken(It.IsAny<Guid>(), It.IsAny<Guid>(), It.IsAny<string>()),
    Times.Once());
grounding_evidence.reason: Added unit test locks in the kill-switch behavior that keeps updating tokens even for terminal status.
Added unit test proves token-update exceptions are swallowed on the kill-switch path and the request still succeeds.
The existing null-input test was flipped to expect RuntimeAssertException, locking in the new exception contract.

scenario_sketches.title: Deletes cached tokens for terminal DAG statuses when the kill switch is off
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover the terminal-status partition defined by Completed/Failed/Skipped/Cancelled and verify GetDAGExecStatus deletes the cached token instead of updating it when the new cleanup behavior is active.

scenario_sketches.title: Refreshes cached tokens for non-terminal DAG executions when the kill switch is off
scenario_sketches.rationale: Lock the non-terminal branch so a running DAG still refreshes its cached token and does not get evicted prematurely.

scenario_sketches.title: Keeps legacy token updates for terminal DAG executions when the kill switch is on
scenario_sketches.rationale: Toggle the feature flag to the opposite state and verify terminal executions revert to the documented old behavior of updating instead of deleting.

scenario_sketches.title: Returns success when UpdateCachedToken throws on the kill-switch path
scenario_sketches.rationale: Inject an UpdateCachedToken failure while the feature flag is enabled and verify the local catch preserves the 200/OK scheduler-status response.

scenario_sketches.title: Throws RuntimeAssertException for null dagExecInstance in GetSchedulerRunStatus
scenario_sketches.rationale: The implementation now uses Check.AssertArgumentNotNull and the existing test expectation was flipped, so the null-input failure type should be locked to RuntimeAssertException.
